title: agoseris glauca var. agrestis
intro: Agoseris glauca var. agrestis, also known as desert helianthus or greasewood hawk's beard, is a rare species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Agoseris glauca var. agrestis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
